papers.name: Paper 1
papers.coverage_notes: Topics 4.1-4.4: Cell biology, Organisation, Infection and response, Bioenergetics. Foundation and Higher Tier.

papers.name: Paper 2
papers.coverage_notes: Topics 4.5-4.7: Homeostasis and response, Inheritance variation and evolution, Ecology. Foundation and Higher Tier.

assessment_objectives.name: Knowledge and understanding
assessment_objectives.description: Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of scientific ideas, techniques and procedures.

assessment_objectives.name: Application
assessment_objectives.description: Apply knowledge and understanding of scientific ideas, enquiry, techniques and procedures.

assessment_objectives.name: Analysis and evaluation
assessment_objectives.description: Analyse information and ideas to interpret and evaluate, make judgements and draw conclusions, develop and improve experimental procedures.

working_scientifically.overview: Working scientifically underpins all specification content and is assessed through WS1-WS4.

areas.name: Development of scientific thinking
areas.description: Scientific explanations, theories, models, peer review, risk, ethics, applications.

areas.name: Experimental skills and strategies
areas.description: Planning, variables, apparatus, methods, measurements, fair tests, hazards.

areas.name: Analysis and evaluation
areas.description: Presenting data, graphs, calculations, statistics, uncertainties, conclusions, evaluation.

areas.name: Scientific vocabulary, quantities, units, symbols and nomenclature
areas.description: Correct use of SI units, prefixes, biological terminology and notation.

working_scientifically.notes: WS/MS/AT references appear in the right column of the two-column specification; they are skill links, not standalone content topics.

required_practicals.notes: Required practical activities are embedded in subject content and listed in Section 8 of the specification. They provide contexts for WS2 and WS3 assessment.
required_practicals.foundation_higher: All required practicals are common to Foundation and Higher Tier. Questions may be set in unfamiliar contexts.

tiers_and_grades.higher: Grades 4-9 (allowed grade 3 if just below boundary)
tiers_and_grades.notes: Tier must be known for item routing; Foundation items may also appear on Higher papers but Higher-only content is not assessed at Foundation.

command_words.notes: Command words are defined in the specification and affect AO demand. Classify items by the command word as well as the content.

difficulty_ladder.description: Single concept recall or direct application; often one-step calculations or naming structures/processes.
Multi-step application within one topic; explain a process, interpret a graph, or apply a practical method.
Cross-topic reasoning, evaluation of evidence, or unfamiliar application of practical skills.

lesson_use.notes: v1 skill supports classification and qbank routing only. Teacher/student/parent modes are not enabled.

exclude_from_questions: AQA Combined Science: Trilogy content that is not part of GCSE Biology 8461
Human Biology / AQA GCSE Biology Higher Tier only content when generating Foundation items
Unofficial command words or mark schemes

marking_guidance.notes: Command words are defined in the specification. Use generated marking guidance only; do not copy official mark schemes.
